title: Humboltia pedunculata
intro: Humboldtia pedunculata, also known as the 'Agnihotra tree', is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, native to the tropical forests of the Western Ghats in India. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. Bioactive constituents of Humboldtia pedunculata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as decoctions and infusions, and is also used in traditional medicine to make formulations like powders, capsules, and tablets.
uses: Humboldtia pedunculata has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, particularly in southern India. The plant is said to possess antifungal, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which contribute to its medicinal uses. It is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and skin infections. The plant is also believed to have antiseptic and antiparasitic properties, making it useful for wound healing and treating internal parasites.
